title: Tangy Flaky Saudi Brisket Stew
description: A slowbraised brisket simmered in a tangy tamarindlemon sauce, soft and flaky with a burst of Middle Eastern spices.
instructions: Pat brisket dry and season with salt, pepper, cumin, and coriander.
Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven over medium‑high heat; brown brisket on all sides, 3‑4 minutes per side.
Add onion to the same pot and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in tomato puree, tamarind paste, and lemon juice; cook 2 minutes to meld flavors.
Deglaze with beef broth, scraping up browned bits; bring to a gentle simmer.
Return brisket to the pot, cover, and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2 to 3 hours, or until the meat is tender and flakes easily with a fork.
Remove from oven, skim excess fat if desired, taste and adjust seasoning.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro, alongside flatbread or rice.
